content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN and an array containing NNN integers.

For each element in the array, you have to output its frequency in the array using Hashing.

The frequency of an element in the array tells how many times it occurs in the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN denoting the number of elements in the array.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers, denoting the elements in the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space separated integers denoting the frequency of each element of the array.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: In this array 111 occurs 444 times, 222 occurs 333 times, 333 occurs 222 times and 444 occurs 111 times.
Test Case 2: In this array 111 occurs 555 times.
Test Case 3: In this array 111 occurs 333 times and 222 occurs 222 times.

solution.approach:
Find the maximum element in the array.
Declare a Hash array of size `max_element + 1`, and initialize all elements to `0`.
Iterate through the array and for each element increment the corresponding index in the Hash array.
solution.timeComplexity: We are finding the maximum element by iterating through the whole array O(N). Then we add the array values to their corresponding indexes into the hash array which takes O(N) time. Total time complexity comes as 2*O(N) -> O(N)
solution.spaceComplexity: The hash array is created which is of size max_element+1. If max_element is M, then this array requires O(M) space.
